fallbackIntro: I don't have a prepared answer for that in my portfolio data yet. I only respond from verified CV and project information — I won't invent experience.

prompts.question: What makes you a senior frontend engineer?
prompts.answer:
Senior work for me means owning maintainability, accessibility, performance, design consistency, and API integration — not just shipping features. Over 10+ years I've led frontend at Qualmity (Health & Safety & compliance B2B apps), Sembo (travel platform rebuild), Signum/DISA (design system across 28 apps), and delivered enterprise UI at IBM iX/Aperto.

I mentor junior developers, conduct code reviews, and make architectural decisions that keep products scalable after launch — component-based architecture, typed contracts, and clear collaboration with design and backend teams.

prompts.question: Tell me about your experience with React and Next.js.
prompts.answer:
React has been central across Qualmity (compliance & screening apps), Sembo (travel platform), Signum/DISA, Eyevinn (video admin tools), and IBM iX/Aperto (enterprise client projects with VEAMS and React).

With Next.js specifically at Sembo I led a corporate travel platform rebuild integrated with Sanity CMS — improving performance by 65% and reducing bounce rates by 48%. At Qualmity I use Next.js with Django REST APIs for compliance and pre-employment screening workflows. I work with SSR/SSG patterns, route-level data fetching, and performance-conscious component architecture.

prompts.question: What is your TypeScript experience?
prompts.answer:
I use TypeScript daily for component props, API response shapes, shared utilities, and design-system contracts across React and Next.js projects at Qualmity, Sembo, and Signum. Strong typing helps catch integration issues early — especially when connecting Next.js frontends to Django REST APIs or evolving component libraries.

I'm pragmatic: strict where it prevents bugs, flexible where it speeds delivery, always aiming for types that make the UI layer easier for the team to maintain.

prompts.question: How do you approach frontend architecture?
prompts.answer:
I start by identifying reusable patterns, shared components, data flow, routing needs, and how UI state maps to backend data. At Qualmity I switched a Health & Safety platform to component-based architecture with a reusable design system (~40% faster page loads). At Signum I architected a modular design system supporting multiple frameworks and helped migrate monoliths toward microservices (quarterly to biweekly releases).

In React/Next.js I focus on clear component boundaries, typed props, predictable API handling, and structures that support future features without becoming fragile.

prompts.question: How do you work with APIs on the frontend?
prompts.answer:
I treat API integration as part of the UI experience. At Qualmity I work closely with backend developers integrating Next.js with Django REST APIs — improving error handling, loading states, and data flows across orders, uploads, certificates, reporting, and dashboard pages.

At Eyevinn I collaborated on REST APIs for video monitoring and admin tools. I align on response shapes early, handle partial/slow data gracefully, and use typed contracts so components stay resilient in production.

prompts.question: How do you turn Figma designs into production-ready interfaces?
prompts.answer:
I map design tokens first — color, typography, spacing, breakpoints — then build components that match the system's intent, not just one screen. At Signum/DISA I reduced design-to-development handoff time by 50% through a modular design system. At Sembo I worked with designers and marketers in workshops and sprint planning to ship a travel platform with consistent UI.

With a UI design background, I clarify interaction states, responsive behavior, accessibility, and edge cases that designs don't always show explicitly.

prompts.question: What is your experience with design systems and component libraries?
prompts.answer:
At Sembo I introduced a design system that reduced component development time by 40%. At Signum/DISA I architected a modular system used across 28 client applications with Bootstrap and TailwindCSS, cutting design-to-dev handoff by 50%. At Qualmity I built a reusable design system for a Health & Safety platform with client-specific branding flexibility.

At IBM iX/Aperto I worked with documented component libraries and the VEAMS framework. I think in terms of clear component APIs, composable patterns, Storybook-friendly documentation, and versioning.

prompts.question: How closely can you match a Figma design?
prompts.answer:
I translate Figma designs closely into production UI when the design system is clear — paying attention to spacing, typography, responsive behavior, interaction states, and WCAG considerations. At Signum my UI design role combined with frontend delivery helped align design intent with scalable implementation across many client applications.

I focus on pixel-faithful UI where it matters, but prioritize consistency, accessibility, and maintainability over one-off screen matching.

prompts.question: What enterprise applications have you built?
prompts.answer:
Enterprise B2B work spans Qualmity (Health & Safety, Risk Management, Pre-Employment Screening, Compliance), Sembo (corporate travel platform), Signum/DISA (compliance apps across 28 clients), IBM iX/Aperto (5+ large client projects), and Eyevinn (B2B video admin portals).

These products involve complex workflows, role-based interfaces, dashboards, forms, document handling, and daily operational use. My focus is making dense interfaces clearer, more consistent, and more reliable for the people using them every day.

prompts.question: What projects have you worked on in travel or compliance?
prompts.answer:
Travel: At Sembo (Jan 2023 – Mar 2024) I led redevelopment of the corporate travel platform with Next.js and Sanity — 65% performance improvement, 48% lower bounce rate, mobile load times from 3.2s to 1.8s.

Compliance: At Signum/DISA (Nov 2020 – Dec 2022) I modernized compliance applications and a company-wide design system. At Qualmity (2024–present) I work on compliance and pre-employment screening apps used daily by analysts — orders, uploads, certificates, reporting, and dashboards integrated with Django REST APIs.

prompts.question: What health and safety software experience do you have?
prompts.answer:
At Qualmity (May 2024 – Present) I lead frontend development for a configurable Health & Safety platform managing driver, vehicle, and operational safety requirements across departments. I moved it to component-based architecture with a reusable design system, reducing page load times by approximately 40% while supporting client-specific branding.

Earlier, at IBM iX/Aperto, I digitized Cemex Health & Safety field inspections in Germany — an admin dashboard for inspections on client sites and cement trucks.

prompts.question: Tell me about your video and media frontend work.
prompts.answer:
At Eyevinn Technology (May – Oct 2020) in Stockholm I built frontend tools for video streaming platforms — a monitoring dashboard used by major media companies, a stream simulation tool (Stream Corruptor) to reproduce network traffic conditions, Consuo (VOD-to-linear stitching library landing page), and optimized React components for a B2B admin portal.

I also created reusable data visualization components for real-time media analytics, collaborating with backend developers on REST API integration in a Kanban agile environment.

prompts.question: How do you improve frontend performance?
prompts.answer:
Measured results from my CV: at Sembo I improved site performance by 65%, reduced bounce rates by 48%, and cut mobile load times from 3.2s to 1.8s. At Qualmity, component-based architecture and a reusable design system reduced page load times by ~40%.

My approach includes bundle optimization, lazy loading, code splitting, Core Web Vitals monitoring, Lighthouse audits, and render-efficient React patterns — choosing the right data-fetching strategy in Next.js and avoiding unnecessary re-renders.

prompts.question: How do you approach accessibility and WCAG?
prompts.answer:
Accessibility is part of how I build — semantic HTML, meaningful headings, keyboard-friendly interactions, visible focus states, and ARIA only when native elements aren't enough. At IBM iX/Aperto I implemented components achieving WCAG 2.1 AA compliance across enterprise client projects.

I also hold IBM Enterprise Design Thinking Practitioner and Interaction Design Foundation HCI certifications. I test with keyboard navigation and screen readers during development, not as a late audit.

prompts.question: What testing experience do you have with Jest, React Testing Library, or Cypress?
prompts.answer:
At Sembo I implemented Jest unit testing and Cypress end-to-end testing for the travel platform rebuild. I use Jest and React Testing Library focusing on user-facing behavior — critical paths, forms, accessibility roles, and regression-prone edge cases.

My CV also lists TDD as a practice. I aim for enough coverage to ship confidently without brittle implementation-detail tests.

prompts.question: How do you collaborate with designers, backend engineers, and product managers?
prompts.answer:
Cross-functional collaboration is consistent across my roles: at Sembo with designers, marketers, and backend in Kanban; at Qualmity with marketing and backend on REST APIs; at IBM iX/Aperto in Design Thinking workshops and Scrum teams; at Signum coordinating external teams across 28 applications.

I review Figma early, align API contracts with backend engineers, translate business goals with product stakeholders, and document decisions in tickets and PRs for async remote work across Germany, Sweden, and international teams.

prompts.question: What is your experience working in distributed teams?
prompts.answer: I've worked remotely and hybrid across Berlin, Gothenburg, Stockholm, and international organizations — Qualmity (remote), Sembo (remote Sweden), Eyevinn (hybrid/remote), and Signum (hybrid Berlin). I'm comfortable with async communication, clear tickets, focused PRs, and collaborating across time zones in Kanban and Scrum environments.

prompts.question: What selected work is in your portfolio?
prompts.answer:
My published case studies cover work across travel, compliance, enterprise agency clients, video/media tooling, and Health & Safety:

• Sembo — travel marketplace / Next.js rebuild
• Signum — compliance platform & design system
• Signum Consulting — company website rebuild
• Qualmity — Health & Safety / screening products
• IBMiX/Aperto — enterprise agency work (plus DIHK, BTV, Consus, Cemex)
• Eyevinn — Video Dev Team, Stream Corruptor, Consuo

Each case study includes role, team context, tools, and outcomes. Ask for a project name to open the matching case study.
